text.title: Design Review Facilitation — Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules for running the 60-minute weekly design review so it produces decisions, not vibes. Each rule has a known violation pattern.

rule.statement: The review MUST end within 60 minutes (hard cap). Agenda allocation: 5 min intake recap, 35-40 min structured critique (time-boxed per artifact based on stage — early-explore: 5 min, refined-comp: 10 min, pre-handoff: 15 min), 10 min decisions, 5-10 min action capture. Overflow goes to next week's review, NOT extended time.

rationale.source: Cal Newport deep-work research, Jake Knapp Sprint methodology

rule.rationale: Hour-plus reviews show steeply declining attention quality. Hard cap forces ruthless prioritization at intake. Overflow protocol prevents the cap from being an aspiration — the review actually ends.

rule.statement: Every piece of feedback MUST be tagged as one of: CLARIFYING ("what does this state represent?"), SUGGESTING ("what if you tried X?"), BLOCKING ("this violates ADR-007 / cannot ship as-is because Y"). Forbidden: "looks great" / "I don't love it" / "feels off" — vague feedback is rejected by the facilitator with a prompt to retag.

rationale.source: Adam Connor — Discussing Design, Discussing Design book research

rule.rationale: Vague feedback wastes time and doesn't change designs. Tagged feedback creates predictable interactions: clarifying = info; suggesting = optional consideration; blocking = must address. Designers respond differently to each, and review proceeds faster.

rule.statement: Every artifact discussed MUST end with one of: DECISION (specific path forward, owner, date), DEFER (named follow-up: who decides, by when, with what new input), or KILL (explicit no-go with reason). Forbidden: "let's think about it" / "let's iterate" without owner + date.

rationale.source: GTD action capture, design-ops process research

rule.rationale: "Let's iterate" produces zero accountability. Explicit decision / defer / kill creates a record that the artifact has been HANDLED. Without it, the same artifact comes back next week with the same questions.

rule.statement: A named scribe captures decisions + actions IN THE MEETING, in real time, in a shared doc visible to attendees. The scribe reads back captures at end of each artifact for confirmation. Forbidden: facilitator-writes-notes-from-memory-after-meeting.

rationale.source: Meeting effectiveness research, post-meeting memory studies

rule.rationale: Post-meeting notes from memory lose 40-60% of decisions and most action-owner assignments. In-meeting capture with live readback catches misinterpretations while everyone is still present.

rule.statement: A recap email / message MUST go out within 24 hours of the meeting with: decisions list (per artifact), action items (with owner + date), defer list (with follow-up trigger), and link to the meeting record. Recipients: all attendees + any cross-functional stakeholders affected.
rule.rationale: Decisions decay without artifact. 24h recap creates the durable record that downstream teams reference. Missing the 24h window correlates with attendees disputing the decisions next week, defeating the meeting's purpose.

example.title: Tagged feedback + clean decision
example.code:
Artifact: "Onboarding screen v3" Stage: refined-comp (10-min time-box) Designer: jane.s
Facilitator opens: "Goals: confirm primary CTA copy + decide on field-order. Anything else is suggesting, not blocking."
Feedback round (round-robin, tagged): alex.k: CLARIFYING — "is the secondary action a button or link?" jane.s answers: it's a link, not a button. mark.p: SUGGESTING — "what if 'Continue' were 'Start free trial'?" jane.s: noted; will A/B in a future test. pat.r: BLOCKING — "the 'Email' field is below 'Workspace name', but workspace creation requires email auth — order is wrong." jane.s: agreed, will swap. liam.h: SUGGESTING — "could the illustration be smaller?" jane.s: noted; will park unless it impacts heat-map data.
Decision (captured by scribe, read back at end): - Decision: swap field order so Email comes first. Owner: jane.s. Date: 2026-05-22 (before handoff). - Defer: copy A/B for primary CTA. Owner: mark.p. Trigger: when onboarding-conversion baseline is stable post-launch.
Time used: 9.5 min (within 10-min box).
example.note: Illustrates r2, r3, r4: tagged feedback, clean decision/defer split, scribe captured with readback.

example.title: LGTM cascade + no decisions
example.code:
Artifact: "Onboarding screen v3" Time: 25 min (over allocated 10 min)
Feedback: alex.k: "looks great" mark.p: "I like it, maybe the button could be different?" pat.r: "feels off but I can't put my finger on it" liam.h: "love the illustration"
End of artifact discussion: "let's iterate." No owner, no date, no scribe notes.
Two weeks later: same artifact in review, same vague feedback, same lack of decision. Designer frustrated; team time wasted.
Repair: enforce r1-r4. Vague feedback gets retagged on the spot; "let's iterate" gets converted to "decision: jane.s revises X by Y".
example.note: Illustrates r1-r4 violations — the standard zombie-review pattern.
